_comment: Ground truth for the pinned evaluation testbed (evaluation/docker-compose.yml).
Every open port + service name + version below is fixed by the pinned container
images, so detection_benchmark.py can compute true precision/recall — nothing is
assumed. 'version' is the version TOKEN the reported banner must contain (e.g.
'2.4.49'); omit it where nmap cannot reliably fingerprint a version (e.g. Postgres
behind auth) so that port is service-scored but not version-scored — never penalise
what we don't assert. 'planted_cves' lists CVEs a version-accurate scanner MUST
recall on that host. Any reported CVE not planted is surfaced as 'unexpected' for
operator review; on a rolling image an unexpected CVE may be genuine, so it is
never auto-scored as a false positive (the offline false-positive guarantee is
measured separately and deterministically by evaluation/cve_precision.py).
'decoy_ports' are ports the scan probes but that are CLOSED, so reporting one as
open is a hard false positive (scored in port precision). Host 172.28.0.15 runs
redis on a NON-STANDARD port (1999) to prove detection follows the banner, not the

hosts.name: redis-nonstd
hosts.note: non-standard port: detection must identify redis by banner, not by port 6379
